subject: Project Management
title: Joseph Juran's Quality Trilogy
description: A 60-second lesson on Joseph Juran's Quality Trilogy.

hook.text: Joseph Juran argued that 80% of defects come from 20% of causes — the Pareto principle applied to quality.

buildup.text: His Quality Trilogy divided quality management into three stages: planning, control, and improvement.

discovery.text: Juran showed that quality isn't just a factory problem — it starts with how products are designed and planned.

twist.text: Like Deming, Juran was first embraced by Japan. American industry caught up decades later.

climax.text: Juran's insight that quality must be planned — not inspected — shaped every modern quality system.

punchline.text: Quality isn't checked at the end. It's built from the start.

quiz.question: What are the three stages of Juran's Quality Trilogy?
